title: virola guatemalensis
intro: Virola guatemalensis is a plant species belonging to the family Myristicaceae, commonly known as the Guatemalan shansho or Guatemalan cowage. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Central America, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Virola guatemalensis include its ability to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its high content of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. Virola guatemalensis is often prepared as a tea or infused in oil for topical application, although its preparation and use should be carefully considered due to potential toxicity and regulatory restrictions.
